MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Suzanne E. Rischman of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Underground Construction Company, Inc.
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Mary Martinez, as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of David Martinez, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT UNDERGROUND CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Underground Construction Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent David Martinez, Sr. was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiff David Martinez, Jr. does not possess knowledge that defendant's employees cut and/or beveled asbestos cement pipe in decedent's presence. The excerpts of David Martinez, Jr.'s deposition testimony submitted with defendant's moving papers address only one specific job site. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Additionally, defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Kyle Dotson are sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: David A. Disbrow, CSR #7768 (Tel: 415-298-1538) (503/CML)
